book_summary: Joshua is complete across all 24 chapters in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The book covers succession after Moses, courage under Torah, Rahab's agency, Jordan crossing, memorial stones, circumcision and Passover, the commander's No/Neither, Jericho, Achan, Ai, Gibeonite survival and forced labor, southern and northern campaigns, the Scroll of Jashar sun-and-moon poem, defeated-kings list, unconquered territories, land allotments, daughters of Zelophehad, Achsah's water request, cities of refuge, Levite cities, the east-Jordan altar dispute and successful de-escalation, Joshua's farewell warnings, Shechem covenant renewal, choose-this-day, covenant stone, Joshua's death, Joseph's burial, and Eleazar's death. Safeguards reject colonial conquest, genocide, child killing, anti-sex-worker stigma, forced genital modification, leader worship, collective punishment, celestial pseudoscience, prisoner humiliation, animal cruelty, Divine-hardening excuses, slavery, forced labor, modern border weaponization, ageism, ableism, patriarchal treatment of women as rewards, ethnic-purity readings, anti-intermarriage ideology, religious persecution, and administrative erasure of living communities.

chapters.notes: Manasseh inheritance, daughters of Zelophehad as legal agents, Canaanite persistence, forced labor, Joseph land complaint, forest-clearing, and iron-chariot resistance.
Shiloh assembly, tent of meeting, seven-tribe survey, written land portions, lot-casting before YHWH, Benjamin borders, and Jebusite Jerusalem in the city list.
Simeon within Judah, remaining tribal allotments, Dan's Leshem seizure and renaming, completion of Shiloh distribution, and Joshua receiving Timnath-serah last.
Cities of refuge, unintentional killing, blood-avenger restraint, city-gate hearing, trial before congregation, high-priest death marker, and equal access for sojourner and resident alien.

Levitical petition for cities and pasturelands, Aaronite/Kohathite/Gershonite/Merarite distribution, refuge cities embedded in Levite network, forty-eight-city total, and fulfillment/rest formula under tension.
East-Jordan tribes released, large Jordan altar, western war mobilization, Phinehas delegation, Peor/Achan memory, witness-altar explanation, and successful de-escalation.
Joshua's first farewell address, remaining nations, Torah adherence, covenant love, non-assimilation warning, intermarriage danger language, fulfillment testimony, and loss warning.

Shechem covenant renewal, ancestors serving other gods, Abraham-to-Exodus-to-land retelling, choose-this-day, Joshua's warning against cheap vows, covenant stone, Joshua and Eleazar deaths, and Joseph's bones buried.

translation_notes: Joshua is complete but remains morally unresolved: its public-memory, refuge, inheritance, and de-escalation traditions coexist with conquest, massacre, forced labor, and displacement.
Rahab, the Gibeonites, the daughters of Zelophehad, Achsah, the refuge cities, and the altar-dispute delegation are key counter-threads of agency, survival, access, asylum, and de-escalation.
Protection agreements remain binding even when powerful parties later resent the conditions under which they were made.
Gibeonite and Gezerite survival through forced labor is not benevolent inclusion; survival without freedom is not justice.
Land allotment registers are ancient administrative and theological claims, not modern title deeds or permission to displace current inhabitants.
The book repeatedly admits remaining peoples and unconquered land, complicating its own total-conquest formulas.
The daughters of Zelophehad continue Torah's gender-inheritance correction and must not be erased in land-law summaries.
Achsah's request for springs shows that land without water and resources is not life-giving inheritance.
Refuge cities establish equal asylum access for Israelite, sojourner, and resident alien before revenge can kill.
Levite cities show that religious vocation still needs material housing, pasture, and communal support.
Joshua 22 offers a rare anti-war model inside Joshua: investigate, listen, accept a peaceful explanation, and stop civil war before it begins.
Joshua 23's intermarriage and foreign-god warnings are covenant-boundary rhetoric, not permission for ethnic-purity ideology, racism, xenophobia, or coercive control of intimate relationships.
Joshua 24 remembers Israel's own ancestors serving other gods, blocking claims of original ethnic spiritual purity.
Choose-this-day is accountable covenant choice, not coercive conversion or a fear-based altar-call formula.
The book closes through burials and continuity after leaders, not conquest alone.
Mystical companion layers remain interpretive and never replace restored/source-audited text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Joshua 17 Zelophehad daughters, Manasseh boundaries, Canaanite persistence, and forced-labor terms require Numbers cross-reference and labor-history audit.
Joshua 18 Shiloh, land survey, written city-portions, Benjamin borders, and Jebus/Jerusalem terminology require archaeological and textual audit.
Joshua 19 Simeon-within-Judah, city totals, Dan/Leshem tradition, and Timnath-serah require textual-witness and Judges comparison.
Joshua 20 refuge-city law requires comparison with Exodus 21, Numbers 35, Deuteronomy 4 and 19, plus asylum and due-process analysis.
Joshua 21 Levite city lists require comparison with 1 Chronicles 6 and textual witnesses for variant names and totals.
Joshua 21 fulfillment/rest formula requires canonical tension audit with remaining-inhabitants notices in Joshua and Judges.
Joshua 22 altar terminology, Geliloth/Gilead geography, Phinehas role, Peor/Achan allusions, and witness naming require textual and intertribal-conflict analysis.
Joshua 23 remaining-nations, intermarriage warning, and good/evil word fulfillment formula require anti-ethnic-purity and anti-persecution safeguards.
Joshua 24 ancestral-god memory, Shechem covenant setting, hornet imagery, qesitah reference, covenant stone, Joseph's bones, and burial notices require source, archaeology, and reception audit.

Corpus-level Joshua audit should compare conquest summaries with allotment contradictions, archaeology of Jericho/Ai/Hazor, Septuagint variants, Samaritan/Gerizim traditions, Judges overlap, and ethical metadata before any status promotion.
next_actions: Begin Judges in a large batch with chapters 1-5: post-Joshua incomplete conquest, Adoni-bezek mutilation, Achsah reprise, forced labor, angel of YHWH at Bochim, cyclical oppression theology, Othniel, Ehud, Shamgar, Deborah and Barak, Jael's killing of Sisera, and the Song of Deborah—with anti-violence, anti-mutilation, anti-slavery, anti-victim-blaming, gender-agency, war-poetry, and trauma safeguards.
